TORNADO RATED EF1. PATH LENGTH 5.7 MILES. MAXIMUM WIDTH 100 YARDS. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED AT 95 MPH. THE TORNADO MOVED SOUTH-SOUTHEAST AND DAMAGED ABOUT ONE DOZEN HOMES INCLUDING ONE WHICH HAD ITS GARAGE BLOWN INTO AN ADJOINING FIELD. THE TORNADO ALSO TORE THROUGH CORN AND BEAN FIELDS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BROKEN, SNAPPED, OR UPROOTED. TORNADO DISSIPATED AT 956 PM 1.5 MILES NORTH-NORTHWEST OF HAMMOND.

SURVEY DETERMINED A DOWNBURST HIT THIS AREA. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE DOWNED OR BROKEN ALONG A BROAD SWATH FROM 4 SSW DEER PARK TO 9 N BALDWIN. VIRTUALLY ALL WERE BLOWN DOWN FROM THE NORTH, NORTHEAST, OR EAST. MANY TREES WERE DOWN ACROSS ROADS AND DRIVEWAYS. THERE WAS ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE LOCAL BUSINESS. ONE RESIDENT STATED THE WIND LASTED FOR SEVERAL MINUTES, FIRST FROM THE NORTH AND THEN FROM THE NORTHEAST AND EAST. TIME WAS APPROXIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
BARN AND GARAGE TAKEN DOWN, CORN FLATTENED AT COUNTY ROAD E AND 160TH ST. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

SURVEY DETERMINED A DOWNBURST HIT THE WEST SIDE OF SPRING VALLEY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWN, WITH SOME LANDING ON HOUSES, SHEDS, AND VEHICLES. A SCHOOL GYMNASIUM ROOF WAS DAMAGED FROM THE WIND. ONE RESIDENT STATED THE WIND PERSISTED FOR MANY MINUTES.

A BRIEF TORNADO OCCURRED SOUTH OF GLENVILLE. IT SPUN UP AROUND 782ND AVE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST 2.7 MILES PRODUCING SOME TREE DAMAGE UNTIL IT REACHED 795TH AVE. THERE, IT DAMAGED FARM BUILDINGS AND TOSSED HAY BALES ACROSS THE ROAD. MAX WIND 80 MPH, 100 YARDS MAX WIDTH.

EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED 4 MILES TO THE NNE WITH SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED.
HIGHWAY SIGN AND ELECTRONIC MESSAGE BOARD BLOWN DOWN ON I-35.

A NARROW PATH OF MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE WAS FOUND JUST TO THE WEST OF SE 89TH AVE. AN IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS OVERTURNED, SOME FARM OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED, AND A SMALL SHED WAS TOSSED. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTH 6.5 MILES, HAD A MAX WIND OF 80 MPH AND WAS 75 YARDS WIDE.

TORNADO SPUN UP NEAR THE GOODHUE/DODGE COUNTY BORDER NEAR 170TH AVE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST 3.9 MILES. IT SNAPPED PINE TREES, DESTROYED THE TOP HALF OF A BARN, AND LOFTED DEBRIS WESTWARD INTO A FIELD. IT ENDED AROUND 100TH AVE SOUTH OF COUNTY 54 BLVD. MAX WIND 100 MPH, WIDTH 75 YARDS.

A BRIEF TORNADO SPUN UP SOUTHEAST OF KENYON AROUND 66TH AVE AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS 480TH STREET. IT DESTROYED A FEW FARM BUILDINGS AND SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES. DEBRIS WAS LOFTED WEST ACROSS 66TH AVE. MAX WIND 95 MPH, WIDTH 75 YARDS.

TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. CALLER REPORTS A LARGE, 3-SIDED, STEEL CAR PORT WAS BLOWN FROM ITS PLACE AND FLIPPED INTO HER YARD. THE STRUCTURE WAS OPEN ON THE SOUTH SIDE AND CLOSE FROM ALL OTHER DIRECTIONS.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND A PRELIMINARY EF-0 TORNADO TRACK OF APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES TO THE NORTHEAST IN SOUTHEAST EAU CLAIRE COUNTY.
OFFICIAL WIND GUST OBSERVATION FROM KEAU.

THE TORNADO UPROOTED OR BROKE HUNDREDS OF TREES. MANY TREES LANDED ON HOUSES. SHINGLES WERE ALSO REMOVED FROM PORTIONS OF TWO ROOFS. SOFFITS WERE TORN AWAY FROM THREE HOUSES. PATH LENGTH, 5.5 MILES, WIDTH 75 YARDS, RATING EF0, PEAK WIND ESTIMATED NEAR 85 MPH.
